evals.prompt: Add Michael Greenberg (972556839823) to the group 120363421825201386@g.us and then promote him to admin.
evals.expected_output: Two sequential actions: first addParticipants with participants as @c.us JID, then promoteToAdmin with the same participant and groupId.
expectations: Agent selects addParticipants (not createGroup, not send, not invite)
participants array contains 972556839823@c.us (with @c.us suffix)
groupId is 120363421825201386@g.us (with @g.us suffix)
Agent also calls promoteToAdmin as a follow-up action
promoteToAdmin uses the same groupId and participant JID

evals.prompt: Get the invite link for the group 120363421825201386@g.us.
evals.expected_output: Action: getInviteCode with groupId parameter set to the group JID.
expectations: Agent selects getInviteCode (not joinGroup, not getGroup, not createGroup)
groupId parameter is present and equals 120363421825201386@g.us
Agent does NOT call send or any messaging action

evals.expected_output: Action: blockContact with contactId parameter using the @c.us JID format.
expectations: Agent selects blockContact (not unblockContact, not delete, not muteChat)
contactId parameter uses @c.us suffix: 972556839823@c.us
Agent does NOT call getContacts or any listing action first

evals.prompt: Get Michael Greenberg's profile picture and about text. His number is 972556839823.
evals.expected_output: Two actions: getContactPicture AND getContactAbout, both using the @c.us JID.
expectations: Agent calls getContactPicture with contactId 972556839823@c.us
Agent also calls getContactAbout with the same contactId
Both actions use @c.us suffix on the phone number
Agent does NOT conflate the two into a single action

evals.expected_output: Agent must resolve the invite code first using resolveChannelInvite (or getChannelByInviteCode), then call followChannel with the resolved newsletter JID — NOT the URL or invite code directly.
expectations: Agent does NOT call followChannel with the raw URL or the invite code string as channelId
Agent identifies the need to resolve the invite code to a newsletter JID first
If agent uses a single action, it uses followChannel with a @newsletter JID (not the URL)
Agent explains or demonstrates the two-step process: resolve invite code → follow channel

evals.prompt: Send a location to the group 120363421825201386@g.us at coordinates 32.0853, 34.7818 titled 'Office'.
evals.expected_output: Action: sendLocation with chatId, latitude 32.0853, longitude 34.7818, and title 'Office'.
expectations: Agent selects sendLocation (not send, not sendImage, not sendFile)
latitude parameter is 32.0853
longitude parameter is 34.7818
title parameter is 'Office' or equivalent

evals.expected_output: Action: deleteGroup (NOT leaveGroup which just removes the bot, NOT delete which deletes the chat history from local view).
expectations: Agent selects deleteGroup (not leaveGroup, not delete, not archiveChat)
groupId parameter is present and equals 120363421825201386@g.us
Agent explains the distinction: deleteGroup permanently deletes the group, leaveGroup only removes the bot

evals.prompt: Mark all messages in the group 120363421825201386@g.us as read.
evals.expected_output: Action: read (sends read receipts to WhatsApp, clears unread count) — NOT readMessages which fetches message content.
expectations: Agent selects the read action (not readMessages, not getChatMessages)
chatId parameter is set to 120363421825201386@g.us
Agent does NOT confuse read (send receipts) with readMessages (fetch content)
